title: neurotheca loeselioides subsp. loeselioides
intro: Neurotheca loeselioides subsp. loeselioides is a plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is known for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Neurotheca loeselioides subsp. loeselioides can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and its extracts have been studied for their potential in developing new herbal remedies.
